title.en: Speaking up when it wasn't going to make you popular
title.es: Hablar cuando no iba a hacerte popular

question.en: Tell me about a time you raised a concern that wasn't going to make you popular. What was at stake, what did you say, and what happened after?
question.es: Cuéntame sobre una ocasión en la que planteaste una preocupación que no iba a hacerte popular. ¿Qué estaba en juego, qué dijiste y qué sucedió después?

must_include: the specific concern raised — named precisely, not summarized as 'I had concerns about the approach'

the concrete language used when speaking up — what the candidate actually said, not paraphrased as 'I expressed my concern' ('I said something like: this feature could mislead customers who…' is the floor)
the named cost the candidate paid or risked paying for speaking up — a specific relationship, timeline, professional standing, or visibility consequence, not 'it was uncomfortable'

the immediate reaction from the person or group the concern was raised with — pushback, silence, defensiveness, or even appreciation — as a factual observation, not a validation ('my manager told me I was overcomplicating it')
an honest account of whether the concern was ultimately addressed — including the possibility that it was dismissed or only partially resolved
must_avoid: vague 'I had a conversation' framing with no specifics on what was actually said — the verbal content is the signal, not the act of having spoken
portraying the decision to speak up as costless or universally applauded — if everyone immediately agreed and nothing was lost, it is not a DTRT story
retroactive ethics: framing the decision as a principled stand after the fact when the concern was actually low-stakes or the outcome was already clear before the candidate spoke
